agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Race condition between stock check and decrement will oversell inventory under concurrent requests"
  },
  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "TOCTOU race condition causes inventory oversell",
      "risk": "Two+ concurrent requests both read sufficient stock, both pass the check, both decrement — resulting in negative stock and oversold inventory",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "addToCart() — gap between getStock() and decrementStock()",
      "reproduction": "Stock=1 for productX. User A and User B both call addToCart(_, productX, 1) simultaneously. Both read stock=1, both pass the check, both decrement. Final stock = -1. Two units sold when only one existed.",
      "fix": "Use an atomic compare-and-decrement operation or wrap the entire function in a serializable transaction. Simplest: replace the read-check-write with a single atomic UPDATE products SET stock = stock - $quantity WHERE id = $productId AND stock >= $quantity, then check rows affected. If 0 rows affected, throw insufficient stock.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Stock integrity — system must never sell more units than exist"
    },
    {
      "title": "Cart and stock updates are not atomic — partial failure leaves inconsistent state",
      "risk": "If addItem succeeds but decrementStock fails (DB error, timeout, crash), the cart contains an item but stock was never decremented. Conversely if decrementStock succeeds but the process crashes before committing, stock is lost with no cart entry.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "addToCart() — two separate awaits with no transaction boundary",
      "reproduction": "Call addToCart. After db.cart.addItem resolves, kill the process or inject a DB connection error. Cart has the item, stock is unchanged. User checks out and buys inventory that was never reserved.",
      "fix": "Wrap addItem and decrementStock in a single database transaction. On any failure, the transaction rolls back both operations atomically.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Data consistency between cart and inventory"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on quantity allows zero/negative values",
      "risk": "quantity=0 passes the stock check (stock >= 0 is always true for valid stock) and adds a zero-quantity cart item. quantity=-5 passes AND increments stock via negative decrement, creating inventory from nothing.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "addToCart() — no validation before stock check",
      "reproduction": "Call addToCart(userId, productId, -10). Stock check: stock < -10 is false for any non-negative stock. decrementStock subtracts -10, effectively adding 10 units. Attacker manufactures infinite inventory.",
      "fix": "Add validation at function entry: if (!Number.isInteger(quantity) || quantity < 1) throw new Error('Invalid quantity');",
      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Input validation — public API must reject malformed input"
    }
  ],
  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No idempotency protection",
      "description": "Retried requests (network timeout, client retry) will add duplicate cart items and double-decrement stock. A public API should accept an idempotency key to make this operation safe to retry.",
      "location": "addToCart() function signature"
    }
  ],
  "suggestions": [
    "Consider SELECT ... FOR UPDATE or advisory locks if your DB supports them, as an alternative to atomic UPDATE",
    "Add a maximum quantity cap to prevent a single user from reserving all stock",
    "Log stock contention events for monitoring popular product bottlenecks"
  ],
  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "Under any concurrent traffic on popular products, stock will go negative. This is not a theoretical risk — it is a guaranteed production failure for any product with more demand than supply.",
    "rollback_capability": "N/A — code review, not a deployment",
    "backward_compatibility": "N/A",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No monitoring for negative stock or partial-failure states. Add alerts for stock < 0.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": false,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": []
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Do not deploy until race condition and atomicity issues are resolved"
  },
  "escalations": [],
  "confidence": {
    "level": 97,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Race condition is deterministic under concurrency — not speculative",
      "Partial failure scenario is straightforward given two non-transactional writes",
      "Negative quantity exploit is trivially reproducible"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [],
    "assumptions": [
      "decrementStock performs stock = stock - quantity without its own >= 0 guard",
      "No external locking or queue sits in front of this function"
    ]
  }
}

content.thinking: Classic TOCTOU race condition in cart stock management.

    },

      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
